task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: If migration runs on read replica or sessions >1000 without manual UPDATE, process_type backfill fails and scoring uses wrong defaults.
task_scores.reasoning: DB writability check, post-migration smoke check, CI migration step, and Step 1.9 prototype scripts reduce prior risks; gt=0 and process_type validation close data integrity gaps.

task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: If active_threshold_spec omits thermal/amps/volts/heat_diss, micro-feedback in Phase 4 falls back to defaults without surfacing the gap.
task_scores.reasoning: Full active_threshold_spec in Step 2.3 ensures micro-feedback receives all fields; optional thresholds param preserves backward compatibility.

task_scores.task_title: Phase 3 — Admin UI & Phase 4 — Micro-Feedback & Report (Steps 3.1–4.6)
task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: If dependency override keys are wrong (routes.thresholds.get_db vs routes.sessions.get_db), test_put_threshold_invalidates_cache hits real DB and passes for wrong reasons.
task_scores.higher_leverage_alternative: Defer Step 4.5 demo callout fallback — admin UI and Replay/WelderReport callouts are the main outcomes.
task_scores.reasoning: Per-generator try/catch in 4.1 and scoreFetchError in 4.2 address empty feedback and silent failure; explicit overrides and session_factory assert in 4.6 close test gaps.

critical_issues: Step 1.2: Migration uses single UPDATE; on 100k+ sessions it may lock or time out before ALTER — large-table guard suggests manual UPDATE first but operator may skip.
Multi-worker cache: In-memory cache is process-local; Gunicorn workers serve stale thresholds until restart — no MVP mitigation beyond documentation.
minor_issues: Step 4.5: Hardcoded 'MIG spec — Target 45° ±5°' fallback is wrong if admin changes MIG seed via UI.
Flicker verification (4.2): Manual throttle test is subjective; no automatable DOM assertion for CI.
suggestions: Add a pre-flight script that runs DB writability check, row-count guard, and exits with clear instructions if sessions >1000.
In Step 4.6, add a comment in the test file documenting that routes.thresholds.get_db and routes.sessions.get_db must both be overridden.
Consider adding a health check or startup log that confirms weld_thresholds has 4 rows after migration.
dependency_order: Phase 1 (1.2 migration) before 1.4 (service) — service needs weld_thresholds. Phase 1 before Phase 2 — Phase 2 needs process_type and threshold_service. Phase 2 before Phase 3/4 — Admin UI and micro-feedback need GET/PUT API and active_threshold_spec in score response. Within Phase 4: 4.1 before 4.2; 4.3/4.4/4.5 after 2.3.
premortem_top_risk: Operator runs alembic upgrade on staging with 1000+ sessions without manual UPDATE first — migration UPDATE blocks and times out, leaving process_type partially NULL.
goal_alignment_notes: Plan delivers 'calibrate to your spec' for procurement: admin configures at /admin/thresholds, scoring and micro-feedback use DB thresholds, reports show spec. Critique fixes (gt=0, process_type validation, DB writability, CI migration, try/catch, scoreFetchError, dependency overrides, session_factory assert) address prior gaps. Visible outcome within 48h: admin can edit and save; Replay and WelderReport show configured spec.
